philosophy

人活着是为了什么

三个答案

  1. 1. 一个依赖
  2. 2. 一个期待
  3. 3. 一个明天

持续寻找

想过什么样的人生，想成为什么样的人，读什么样的书？

史蒂夫·乔布斯在 2005 年斯坦福大学毕业典礼演讲中说得最好：

你必须找到你所爱的。对于你的工作和你的爱人来说都是如此。你的工作将占据你生活的很大一部分，而真正满足的唯一方法就是做你认为伟大的工作。做好工作的唯一方法就是热爱你所做的事情。

如果您还没有找到，请继续寻找。并且不要安定下来。就像所有与内心有关的事情一样，当你找到它时你就会知道。而且，就像任何伟大的关系一样，随着时间的推移，这种关系会变得越来越好。所以继续寻找。别安定下来。

什么是正确的重复？

  • 第一：选对方向，用时间悄悄地做杠杆；
  • 第二：找到优势，用效果不断地做叠加。

content.thinking:
Now I understand the task:
1. Add a philosophy section in `page.tsx` after the Education section - with English content based on the philosophy page content
2. Remove the philosophy nav item from `resume.tsx`
3. Delete (or redirect) the philosophy page at `src/app/philosophy/page.tsx`

Wait, let me re-read: "去掉底部philosophy 的入口和整个页面" - remove the philosophy nav entry and the entire page.

So the tasks are:
1. Add Philosophy section in `page.tsx` after Education, with English content
2. Remove philosophy from `resume.tsx` navbar


3. Delete the philosophy page entirely

Now I'm translating the philosophy content to English - the main sections are "Three Answers" covering what we depend on and look forward to, "Keep Searching" with reflections on life choices and the Steve Jobs Stanford speech, and a third section that appears to be cut off.

For the navbar, I need to remove philosophy from the navigation array in resume.tsx, and I should also check the sitemap and llms.txt files since they likely reference this page. Let me start with the main deletions first.

现在我清楚了，需要做三件事：
1. 在首页 Education 后面加 Philosophy 内容块（英文版）
2. 从 navbar 移除 Philosophy 入口
3. 删除 philosophy 页面

先处理首页和 resume.tsx：

input.new_string:
Philosophy

Three Answers to Why We Live

  1. 1. Something to depend on
  2. 2. Something to look forward to
  3. 3. A tomorrow

Keep Searching

Steve Jobs said it best at the 2005 Stanford commencement:

You've got to find what you love. And that is as true for your work as it is for your lovers. Your work is going to fill a large part of your life, and the only way to be truly satisfied is to do what you believe is great work.

If you haven't found it yet, keep looking. Don't settle. As with all matters of the heart, you'll know when you find it.

What Is Correct Repetition?

  • 1. Choose the right direction — let time silently work as leverage.
  • 2. Find your advantage — keep stacking on the effect.

content.content:
1→import { getBlogPosts } from '@/data/blog';
     2→import { DATA } from '@/data/resume';
     3→
     4→export async function GET() {
     5→  const posts = await getBlogPosts();
     6→  const recentPosts = posts
     7→    .sort(
     8→      (a, b) =>
     9→        new Date(b.metadata.publishedAt).getTime() -
    10→        new Date(a.metadata.publishedAt).getTime()
    11→    )
    12→    .slice(0, 20);
    13→
    14→  const blogList = recentPosts
    15→    .map(
    16→      (post) =>
    17→        `- [${post.metadata.title}](${DATA.url}/blog/${post.slug})${post.metadata.summary ? ': ' + post.metadata.summary : ''}`
    18→    )
    19→    .join('\n');
    20→
    21→  const content = `# ${DATA.name}
    22→
    23→> ${DATA.description}
    24→
    25→${DATA.name} is a Web3 Product Engineer with 6 years of experience building decentralized applications. Founder of iBuidl, a digital nomad community helping developers transition into Web3.
    26→
    27→## About
    28→
    29→${DATA.summary}
    30→
    31→## Pages
    32→
    33→- [Home](${DATA.url}): Portfolio homepage with work experience, projects, and skills overview
    34→- [Blog](${DATA.url}/blog): Articles on Web3, DeFi, crypto investing, blockchain technology, and product engineering
    35→- [Stack](${DATA.url}/stack): Tools, frameworks, and technologies used daily
    36→- [Fund](${DATA.url}/fund): Crypto investment portfolio and fund insights
    37→- [Fear](${DATA.url}/fear): Bitcoin bottom probability dashboard with valuation, on-chain, sentiment, macro, and risk layers
    38→- [Philosophy](${DATA.url}/philosophy): Personal notes about purpose, love, long-term direction, and correct repetition
    39→
    40→## Recent Blog Posts
    41→
    42→${blogList}
    43→
    44→## Skills & Expertise
    45→
    46→${DATA.skills.join(', ')}
    47→
    48→## Projects
    49→
    50→- [Yamaswap](https://yamaswap.com): Permissionless ETFs dApp built on Intent Framework and AI Agent (SOL, BASE)
    51→- [fCurrency](https://farcaster.REDACTED): Composable conversion engine for fiat and crypto assets
    52→- [AnkiRin](https://rin.kkdemian.com/): AI-powered Japanese vocabulary flashcard app
    53→- [IBuidl](https://ibuidl.org/): Web3 digital nomad community and transformation platform
    54→- [Web3 Crypto Tools](https://tool.ibuidl.org/): Cryptocurrency gadgets and mainstream Web3 tools
    55→
    56→## Social & Contact
    57→
    58→- GitHub: https://github.com/chuhemiao
    59→- X (Twitter): https://x.com/0xkkdemian
    60→- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@kkdemian
    61→- Telegram: https://t.me/kkdemian_laobai
    62→- Crypto Book: https://book.kkdemian.com/
    63→- RSS Feed: ${DATA.url}/rss.xml
    64→`;
    65→
    66→  return new Response(content, {
    67→    headers: {
    68→      'Content-Type': 'text/plain; charset=utf-8',
    69→      'Cache-Control': 's-maxage=86400, stale-while-revalidate=604800',
    70→    },
    71→  });
    72→}
    73→

## 1. Executive Summary

This report aims to use a six-dimensional analytical framework to project key structural trends in the crypto market in 2026. This framework integrates the macro-monetary environment, on-chain capital structure, narrative cycles, regulatory frameworks, technological protocol evolution, and market microstructure. We abandon single-point predictions and explore possible market paths under different combinations of variables through three scenarios: baseline, risk, and tail scenarios.

## TL;DR

AsterDEX represents a post-merger perpetual exchange combining yield infrastructure (Astherus) with derivatives trading (APX Finance) into a multi-chain platform featuring up to 1001× leverage and yield-bearing collateral. With $2.4B open interest, $1.3B TVL, and $7-8B daily volume as of December 2025, the platform demonstrates significant traction post-rebrand. However, sustainability concerns arise from 94.58% token concentration in top 10 holders, past wash-trading delisting from DefiLlama, and low daily user retention (6.1% DAU/MAU). Revenue generation ($4,500 daily) significantly exceeds incentives ($40 daily), indicating economic viability, but competitive positioning against established leaders like Hyperliquid ($14B OI) and governance maturity remain developmental.

## TL;DR

Binance remains the dominant global crypto exchange with 300M+ users, $168B+ daily volume (77-81% derivatives), and comprehensive product suite spanning spot, futures, options, and DeFi infrastructure. The platform generated $16.8B revenue in 2024 (+40% YoY) while navigating complex regulatory transitions across 20+ jurisdictions. Despite maintaining 33-40% market share leadership, structural pressure from perp DEXs (Hyperliquid, Lighter, Aster capturing $4-5B daily volume) and regulated competitors signals gradual moat erosion. Strategic pivot to ADGM compliance, dual-CEO governance, and BNB Chain ecosystem expansion positions Binance for sustained defensibility, though capital efficiency advantages and non-custodial trust models of perp DEXs represent long-term margin compression risks.

## Canton Network Privacy-enabled Layer-1

Canton Network is a privacy-enabled Layer-1 blockchain designed for institutional finance, processing $6T+ in on-chain real-world assets and 600,000+ daily transactions as of December 2025. The protocol employs a unique burn-mint tokenomics model with its native CC token (market cap $2.7B, circulating 36B tokens), rewarding validators and application providers through a fair-launch mechanism with no pre-mine or VC allocations. With 575+ validators including major institutions like Goldman Sachs and BNP Paribas, Canton has achieved product-market fit in regulated finance, though its privacy-first architecture limits traditional holder transparency and on-chain analytics.

## TL;DR

Hyperliquid represents the most sophisticated attempt to replicate centralized exchange performance on-chain, achieving sub-second finality and $3.57B daily perpetual volume through a custom L1 architecture. The protocol generated $845.6M cumulative revenue with 93% flowing to HLP liquidity providers and 7% funding HYPE buybacks, creating deflationary pressure via the recently approved $912M burn of Assistance Fund holdings. Technical analysis reveals consolidation near $25 support with short-term bullish momentum (4h MACD +0.074, CMF +0.06) against a broader daily downtrend (RSI 39.8), while sustained positive funding rates and $1.38B open interest signal perp premium pressure. The protocol dominates 60-70% of on-chain perpetual volume with 41,280 DAU and $4.135B TVL, though concentration risks persist with top holders controlling 24-30% of circulation and ongoing monthly unlocks through 2028 adding dilution pressure.

KGST is a newly launched (December 2025) BEP-20 stablecoin pegged 1:1 to the Kyrgyz Som, backed by fiat reserves in licensed Kyrgyz banks and supported by government regulatory framework. Suitable primarily for Kyrgyzstan-focused remittance use cases, but requires caution for broader institutional adoption due to early-stage risks and emerging market exposure.

## TL;DR

The prediction market sector achieved $44B in trading volume during 2025, representing a structural shift from academic curiosity to mainstream financial infrastructure. Two dominant models emerge: CFTC-regulated centralized exchanges (Kalshi: $17.1B volume, $1B funding) and crypto-native decentralized protocols (Polymarket: $21.5B volume, $2.279B funding). Core findings: (1) tokenless models demonstrate superior market traction versus tokenized alternatives, (2) order-book mechanisms dominate despite early LMSR AMM designs, (3) regulatory arbitrage enables growth but creates fragmentation risk, (4) information aggregation outperforms traditional polling in high-liquidity markets but fails during manipulation or thin participation. The sector faces winner-take-most dynamics favoring liquidity concentration, with 73% of DeFi TVL ($423M total) concentrated in Polymarket alone.

## TL;DR

Rainbow Wallet is a design-led, non-custodial Ethereum wallet with strong product-market fit in NFT/L2 UX but modest scale (~1.2M+ monthly active wallets across 9+ chains). Revenue model relies on 0.85% swap fees (~$47k monthly, $2-3M annualized) with upcoming RNBW token (Dec 2025 TGE) enabling governance and buyback mechanics. Strategic moat centers on RainbowKit developer adoption (2.7k+ GitHub stars, integrations across Coinbase, Optimism, Arbitrum) and mobile-first design excellence (4.6/5 App Store rating). Key risks include regulatory exposure, RPC/liquidity dependencies, and limited security audits. Positioned as premium alternative to MetaMask for NFT-native users, with chain abstraction potential via multi-L2 integration.

## TL;DR

The stablecoin sector represents a $310 billion blockchain-native monetary system with 59% YoY growth, dominated by fiat-backed custodial models (95% market share) that function as regulated narrow banks earning $5-6 billion annually from Treasury reserves. While demonstrating payment rail superiority over traditional systems ($8.5 trillion quarterly volume vs Visa's $3.9 trillion), the sector faces structural constraints from regulatory convergence toward centralized licensing, interest rate dependency, and concentration risks (top two issuers control 85% share). The monetary architecture proves resilient under stress—USDC restored peg within days post-SVB exposure—but yield-bearing models like USDe exhibit volatility (-56% supply in 90 days). Strategic outlook favors integration with institutional payments (PayFi) and AI agent economies, positioning stablecoins as durable settlement infrastructure constrained by trust and regulatory boundaries rather than purely transitional instruments.

## TL;DR

The prediction market sector achieved $44B in trading volume during 2025, representing a structural shift from academic curiosity to mainstream financial infrastructure. Two dominant models emerge: CFTC-regulated centralized exchanges (Kalshi: $17.1B volume, $1B funding) and crypto-native decentralized protocols (Polymarket: $21.5B volume, $2.279B funding). Core findings: (1) tokenless models demonstrate superior market traction versus tokenized alternatives, (2) order-book mechanisms dominate despite early LMSR AMM designs, (3) regulatory arbitrage enables growth but creates fragmentation risk, (4) information aggregation outperforms traditional polling in high-liquidity markets but fails during manipulation or thin participation. The sector faces winner-take-most dynamics favoring liquidity concentration, with 73% of DeFi TVL ($423M total) concentrated in Polymarket alone.

summary: 'Aztec represents a fundamental architectural innovation in Ethereum scaling - a privacy-first zk-rollup that enables programmable confidentiality as a native primitive.'
category: 'research'
---

## Executive Summary

Aztec represents a fundamental architectural innovation in Ethereum scaling - a privacy-first zk-rollup that enables programmable confidentiality as a native primitive. Unlike transparent zkEVMs that focus solely on scalability, Aztec's "ZK-ZK rollup" approach delivers both validity proofs and privacy guarantees through its novel hybrid state model and client-side proof generation. [Aztec Network](https://aztec.network/blog/the-best-of-both-worlds-how-aztec-blends-private-and-public-state)

content.content:
# kkdemian

Personal portfolio site for [kkdemian](https://kkdemian.com) — Web3 Product Engineer, digital nomad, Bitcoin & ETH HODL.

Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Deployed on Vercel.

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/chuhemiao/portfolio
   cd portfolio
   ```

2. Install dependencies:

   ```bash
   pnpm install
   ```

3. Start the dev server:

   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```

4. Edit personal data in [`src/data/resume.tsx`](./src/data/resume.tsx)

## Pages

| Route         | Description                            |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `/`           | Home — bio, work, projects, hackathons |
| `/blog`       | Blog posts organized by year/category  |
| `/stack`      | Tools and tech stack                   |
| `/fund`       | Fund tracker                           |
| `/fear`       | Crypto Fear & Greed index              |
| `/philosophy` | Personal philosophy                    |

## Content Management

Blog posts live under `content/blog/` and can be organized in nested folders (e.g. `content/blog/2026/research/`).

**Frontmatter:**

```md
---
title: 'Post title'
publishedAt: '2026-02-25'
summary: 'One line summary'
slug: 'my-post-slug'
category: 'research'
---
```

- Routes are always `/blog/[slug]` regardless of folder location.
- Draft posts: place in a `drafts/` or `_drafts/` folder, or set `draft: true` in frontmatter.

**Useful commands:**

```bash
# Create a new post (auto-generates category, slug, and path)
pnpm new:post -- "A deep research report about BTC and macro liquidity"

# Validate content before publishing
pnpm content:check

# One-time batch organize root-level files into year folders
pnpm content:organize
```

`new:post` supports optional flags: `--title`, `--category`, `--date`, `--slug`, `--dry-run`

## License

MIT

content.thinking:
Now I have a complete picture. I need to:

1. **Update README.md** - update the `/philosophy` page entry to remove it, add `/research`, and add a section listing all research report titles
2. **Create `/research` page** - a visual project map like the image shown, with project logos, one-liner, type, and research link

For the research page, I need to identify specific projects (not market outlook reports) and create cards for them. Looking at the image, it shows projects like Uniswap, Coinbase, Optimism, Phantom, Fireblocks, Kalshi, Flashbots, Privy - each with a logo, name, description, and type.


I'm scanning through the research files to pull out the actual projects I can feature. I can see several exchange platforms like Binance, Coinbase, Bybit, Bitget, Gate.io, MEXC, HashKey, XT.COM, and MSX Exchange that have dedicated research reports. Continuing through the DeFi and infrastructure landscape, I'm cataloging additional perp DEX platforms like Reya Network and StandX, along with yield optimization tools and broader blockchain infrastructure projects spanning Ethereum, Bitcoin, Sui, and various layer 2 solutions with different scaling approaches. Looking at the rest of the privacy and ZK projects, then moving through prediction markets with platforms like Polymarket, Opinion Labs, and several others, and finally into stablecoins and payment infrastructure solutions. I'm seeing more projects to add to the list, though some are duplicates or tool-focused rather than protocol-focused. For the research map, I should prioritize the most impactful projects with dedicated research reports rather than trying to include everything. I'm thinking about the card layout now—each one will display the project logo, name, a brief description, and a category tag to keep things organized and scannable.

# kkdemian

Personal portfolio site for [kkdemian](https://kkdemian.com) — Web3 Product Engineer, digital nomad, Bitcoin & ETH HODL.

Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Deployed on Vercel.

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/chuhemiao/portfolio
   cd portfolio
   ```

2. Install dependencies:

   ```bash
   pnpm install
   ```

3. Start the dev server:

   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```

4. Edit personal data in [`src/data/resume.tsx`](./src/data/resume.tsx)

## Pages

| Route         | Description                            |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `/`           | Home — bio, work, projects, hackathons |
| `/blog`       | Blog posts organized by year/category  |
| `/stack`      | Tools and tech stack                   |
| `/fund`       | Fund tracker                           |
| `/fear`       | Crypto Fear & Greed index              |
| `/philosophy` | Personal philosophy                    |

## Content Management

Blog posts live under `content/blog/` and can be organized in nested folders (e.g. `content/blog/2026/research/`).

**Frontmatter:**

```md
---
title: 'Post title'
publishedAt: '2026-02-25'
summary: 'One line summary'
slug: 'my-post-slug'
category: 'research'
---
```

- Routes are always `/blog/[slug]` regardless of folder location.
- Draft posts: place in a `drafts/` or `_drafts/` folder, or set `draft: true` in frontmatter.

**Useful commands:**

```bash
# Create a new post (auto-generates category, slug, and path)
pnpm new:post -- "A deep research report about BTC and macro liquidity"

# Validate content before publishing
pnpm content:check

# One-time batch organize root-level files into year folders
pnpm content:organize
```

`new:post` supports optional flags: `--title`, `--category`, `--date`, `--slug`, `--dry-run`

## License

MIT

# kkdemian

Personal portfolio site for [kkdemian](https://kkdemian.com) — Web3 Product Engineer, digital nomad, Bitcoin & ETH HODL.

Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Deployed on Vercel.

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/chuhemiao/portfolio
   cd portfolio
   ```

2. Install dependencies:

   ```bash
   pnpm install
   ```

3. Start the dev server:

   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```

4. Edit personal data in [`src/data/resume.tsx`](./src/data/resume.tsx)

## Pages

| Route         | Description                            |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------- |
| `/`           | Home — bio, work, projects, hackathons |
| `/blog`       | Blog posts organized by year/category  |
| `/stack`      | Tools and tech stack                   |
| `/fund`       | Fund tracker                           |
| `/fear`       | Crypto Fear & Greed index              |
| `/philosophy` | Personal philosophy                    |

## Content Management

Blog posts live under `content/blog/` and can be organized in nested folders (e.g. `content/blog/2026/research/`).

**Frontmatter:**

```md
---
title: 'Post title'
publishedAt: '2026-02-25'
summary: 'One line summary'
slug: 'my-post-slug'
category: 'research'
---
```

- Routes are always `/blog/[slug]` regardless of folder location.
- Draft posts: place in a `drafts/` or `_drafts/` folder, or set `draft: true` in frontmatter.

**Useful commands:**

```bash
# Create a new post (auto-generates category, slug, and path)
pnpm new:post -- "A deep research report about BTC and macro liquidity"

# Validate content before publishing
pnpm content:check

# One-time batch organize root-level files into year folders
pnpm content:organize
```

`new:post` supports optional flags: `--title`, `--category`, `--date`, `--slug`, `--dry-run`

## License

MIT

content.text: 确认没有 TS 错误：

headers.cache-control: private, max-age=0, no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate, post-check=0, pre-check=0

content.thinking: The diagnostics are just markdown linting warnings (blank lines around headings/lists) and cSpell warnings for crypto project names. These are cosmetic warnings that don't affect functionality - I don't need to fix them unless the user asks.

content.text:
这些都是 markdownlint 格式警告和 cSpell 对加密项目名称的误报，不影响功能。

完成所有改动，总结如下：

**`/research` 新页面**
- `src/app/research/page.tsx` — 页面元数据
- `src/app/research/research-client.tsx` — 客户端组件，包含 60 个项目的数据和类型筛选器（CEX、Perp DEX、DEX、Lending、L1、ZK、Prediction、Stablecoin、AI/DePIN、RWA 等）

4. **[github.com/coinwink/cryptocurrency-logos](https://github.com/coinwink/cryptocurrency-logos)**
   Contains **8,030 different logos**, all in PNG format in three dimensions: 16×16, 32×32, and 128×128 px. Logos are named according to their CoinMarketCap ID, where Bitcoin's ID is 1 and all other coins get IDs incrementally based on the date they were added to CMC.

results:
Here is a summary of the **CoinMarketCap UCIDs (Unified Cryptoasset IDs)** found from the search results:

---

### ✅ Confirmed UCIDs from CoinMarketCap

| Token | Symbol | UCID | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Sky** (formerly Maker/MKR) | SKY | **33038** | [coinmarketcap.com/currencies/sky/](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/sky/) |
| **Wrapped Centrifuge** | WCFG | **10898** | [coinmarketcap.com/currencies/wrapped-centrifuge/](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/wrapped-centrifuge/) |
| **syrupUSDC** (Maple yield token) | syrupUSDC | **38779** | [coinmarketcap.com/currencies/syrup-usdc/](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/syrup-usdc/) |

---

### ℹ️ Details from Search Results

- **Sky (SKY)** has a UCID of **33038** on CoinMarketCap. Maker (MKR) has been rebranded to Sky (SKY).

- **Wrapped Centrifuge (WCFG)** has a UCID of **10898** on CoinMarketCap.

- **syrupUSDC** (Maple Finance's yield-bearing stablecoin) has a UCID of **38779** on CoinMarketCap.

- The original **Maple (MPL)** token is being migrated to **Maple Finance (SYRUP)**. The UCID for the **SYRUP** governance token itself was not directly returned in the search snippets from its CoinMarketCap page.

- The UCID for the native **Centrifuge (CFG)** token (post-EVM migration) was also not directly surfaced in the search snippets. Over 241 million legacy CFG tokens (wCFG and Substrate-based) were migrated to an EVM-native format on Ethereum by August 5, 2025, allowing holders to participate in cross-chain governance. This migration may mean CMC has separate entries for legacy vs. new CFG.

---

### ⚠️ Not Confirmed in Search Results
- **Centrifuge (CFG)** native UCID — not surfaced in snippets; please check directly at [coinmarketcap.com/currencies/centrifuge/](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/centrifuge/) and look for the "UCID" field on the page.
- **Maple Finance (SYRUP)** governance token UCID — not surfaced in snippets; please check directly at [coinmarketcap.com/currencies/maple-finance/](https://coinmarketcap.com/currencies/maple-finance/).

For the most accurate and up-to-date UCIDs, it's recommended to visit each token's dedicated CoinMarketCap page directly, where the **UCID field** is displayed in the token info section.

Writing the sync script...

Now I'm deciding between three approaches: a script that just reports missing research entries for manual addition, one that both reports and auto-generates scaffold entries with default colors, or something in between. The challenge is that auto-generation would need sensible defaults for color and initial values since we can't know those from the slug alone.

Given the user's request for automatic addition, I should go with the middle ground—create a sync script that reads all MDX files with the research category, identifies which ones aren't in the PROJECTS array yet, and appends scaffold entries with the title as the name and a truncated summary as the description.

For the remaining fields, I'll infer the type from keywords in the title and summary (like "exchange", "trading", "OKX" for CEX), derive the slug from the frontmatter, generate a deterministic color based on a hash of the slug, and pull the initial from the first couple letters of the slug or title.

The trickier part is handling the file format—research-client.tsx uses JS template literals and imports, so I'll need to parse the existing PROJECTS array, find where it ends (likely before the closing `];`), and insert new entries there while preserving the structure. content.thinking:
Good, there are 20 more research posts that aren't in the research-client.tsx. Looking at these, most of them are market analysis/macro posts (Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, crypto market outlooks) rather than specific project deep-dives. But some are actual project research:

1. `definitive-43m-revenue-multi-chain-twap-trading-infrastructure` - Definitive (EDGE): actual project research, should add
2. `kgst_stablecoin` - KGST Stablecoin: actual project research, should add
3. `arc_tempo_stablecoin` - Circle Arc vs Stripe Tempo: comparison, might add


4. `octra_report_2025` - Octra has a different slug in the research page (`octra_fhe_zk`), so this appears to be a separate report
5. `prediction_pmf_2026` - Rise of Prediction Markets: more of a sector report

The remaining posts are mostly market and macro analysis, so I should probably create a new category for those rather than mixing them with project-specific research.

Looking back at what the user actually asked for—syncing newly added research *projects*—I should focus on the ones that are clearly project-focused rather than broad market analyses. So I'll add the four that stand out: Definitive (DeFi terminal), KGST (stablecoin), Arc vs Tempo (stablecoin comparison), and the separate Octra investment research report. the research map too. The script already reports them all, so the user can run the auto-add command if they want to include everything. For now, I'll just add the clearly new projects: Definitive's multi-chain trading infrastructure and the KGST stablecoin. I'll skip the Octra report since that project is already documented.

Actually, I should be more comprehensive here. Bitcoin and ETH reports are also project entries, and they're already in the README, so I might as well add those along with the other genuine projects. The sync script can handle detection, and the user can always use the auto-add flag to scaffold everything and review afterward.

Now I'm deciding which specific slugs to use for Bitcoin and Ethereum—there are multiple report versions available. I'll go with bitcoin_2026_report for Bitcoin and eth_outlook_2026 for Ethereum, then add Definitive, KGST Stablecoin, and check whether sector reports like the Global Stablecoin Report and Prediction Markets PMF should be included as separate entries. I'm also noticing that octra_report_2025 is a duplicate since Octra is already listed, so I'll skip that one. I'm narrowing this down to the core project cards that matter—Bitcoin and Ethereum as the major L1s, plus Definitive and KGST as concrete project examples. The sector and market analysis pieces are useful context but don't belong in the research map itself.

Looking at the README vs the research-client.tsx, I need to:

1. Add to DEX / Lending / DeFi: TradeGenius, MetaDAO, RaveDAO, Definitive
2. Add to L1 / L2 / Infrastructure: Bitcoin, Ethereum (moved from Market Analysis to L1 section since they're now in the research map as L1 projects)
3. Add to Stablecoin / PayFi: River
4. Add to Infrastructure/Infra: Sentio (new Infra section or add to L1/L2/Infrastructure)

Wait, looking at the README more carefully:


- Probable and KGST are already there, so I don't need to add those. But I'm seeing River, TradeGenius, MetaDAO, RaveDAO, and Sentio are in the research-client.tsx but missing from the README, so those are the ones I need to add.

For Bitcoin and Ethereum, they're already in the README under Market Analysis, but now that I've added them to the research map as L1 projects with their own dedicated reports, I should move them to the L1 section instead of keeping them only in Market Analysis. The Market Analysis section is for broader market-level reports, while the L1 section is for specific blockchain projects, and since we have bitcoin_2026_report and eth_outlook_2026, they fit better there. I'm settling on the simplest approach: just add the new entries that aren't already in the README to their appropriate sections without moving anything that's already there. So I need to add River to Stablecoin/PayFi, the three DAOs and TradeGenius to DeFi, and Sentio to Infrastructure.

For Bitcoin and Ethereum, they're already in Market Analysis but should also go in the L1 section since they're now part of the research map. I'll add Sentio to the L1/L2/Infrastructure section rather than creating a separate Infra section, then make the edits to the README.

# kkdemian

Personal portfolio site for [kkdemian](https://kkdemian.com) — Web3 Product Engineer, digital nomad, Bitcoin & ETH HODL.

Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Deployed on Vercel.

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/chuhemiao/portfolio
   cd portfolio
   ```

2. Install dependencies:

   ```bash
   pnpm install
   ```

3. Start the dev server:

   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```

4. Edit personal data in [`src/data/resume.tsx`](./src/data/resume.tsx)

## Pages

| Route        | Description                                              |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `/`          | Home — bio, work, projects, philosophy, hackathons       |
| `/blog`      | Blog posts organized by year/category                    |
| `/stack`     | Tools and tech stack                                     |
| `/fund`      | Fund tracker                                             |
| `/fear`      | Crypto Fear & Greed index                                |
| `/research`  | Deep-dive research map — 60+ crypto project reports      |

## Content Management

Blog posts live under `content/blog/` and can be organized in nested folders (e.g. `content/blog/2026/research/`).

**Frontmatter:**

```md
---
title: 'Post title'
publishedAt: '2026-02-25'
summary: 'One line summary'
slug: 'my-post-slug'
category: 'research'
---
```

- Routes are always `/blog/[slug]` regardless of folder location.
- Draft posts: place in a `drafts/` or `_drafts/` folder, or set `draft: true` in frontmatter.

**Useful commands:**

```bash
# Create a new post (auto-generates category, slug, and path)
pnpm new:post -- "A deep research report about BTC and macro liquidity"

# Validate content before publishing
pnpm content:check

# One-time batch organize root-level files into year folders
pnpm content:organize
```

`new:post` supports optional flags: `--title`, `--category`, `--date`, `--slug`, `--dry-run`

## Research Reports

Deep-dive investment research published at `/research`. Listed by project:

# kkdemian

Personal portfolio site for [kkdemian](https://kkdemian.com) — Web3 Product Engineer, digital nomad, Bitcoin & ETH HODL.

Built with Next.js, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI. Deployed on Vercel.

## Getting Started

1. Clone the repository:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/chuhemiao/portfolio
   cd portfolio
   ```

2. Install dependencies:

   ```bash
   pnpm install
   ```

3. Start the dev server:

   ```bash
   pnpm dev
   ```

4. Edit personal data in [`src/data/resume.tsx`](./src/data/resume.tsx)

## Pages

| Route        | Description                                              |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `/`          | Home — bio, work, projects, philosophy, hackathons       |
| `/blog`      | Blog posts organized by year/category                    |
| `/stack`     | Tools and tech stack                                     |
| `/fund`      | Fund tracker                                             |
| `/fear`      | Crypto Fear & Greed index                                |
| `/research`  | Deep-dive research map — 60+ crypto project reports      |

## Content Management

Blog posts live under `content/blog/` and can be organized in nested folders (e.g. `content/blog/2026/research/`).

**Frontmatter:**

```md
---
title: 'Post title'
publishedAt: '2026-02-25'
summary: 'One line summary'
slug: 'my-post-slug'
category: 'research'
---
```

- Routes are always `/blog/[slug]` regardless of folder location.
- Draft posts: place in a `drafts/` or `_drafts/` folder, or set `draft: true` in frontmatter.

**Useful commands:**

```bash
# Create a new post (auto-generates category, slug, and path)
pnpm new:post -- "A deep research report about BTC and macro liquidity"

# Validate content before publishing
pnpm content:check

# One-time batch organize root-level files into year folders
pnpm content:organize
```

`new:post` supports optional flags: `--title`, `--category`, `--date`, `--slug`, `--dry-run`

## Research Reports

Deep-dive investment research published at `/research`. Listed by project:

### Exchanges (CEX)
- Binance: Institutional-Grade Exchange Research Report
- Coinbase Investment-Grade Research Report (2026)
- Bybit: Liquidity Deep Dive — $22.9B Daily Trading Volume
- Bitget Deep Research Report: Comprehensive Investment Analysis
- Gate.io In-Depth Research Report: Cyclical Value Analysis
- MEXC In-Depth Research Report: Zero-Fee Strategy Analysis
- HashKey Exchange: Compliance-First Architecture for Regulated Markets
- XT.COM Deep Research: Capital Aggregation Infrastructure
- MSX Exchange Deep Research: RWA Tokenization Pioneer
- OSL Digital Financial Infrastructure Investment Research

### Perpetual DEX
- Hyperliquid Investment-Grade Research Report (2025/2026)
- AsterDEX Investment-Grade Research Report
- Reya Network In-Depth Investment Research Report
- StandX Protocol Deep Dive: Yield-Bearing Perpetuals DEX
- Pacifica Finance: Solana's Hybrid Perpetual Exchange Analysis
- Perpl.xyz In-Depth Research: Monad Order Book Perp Protocol
- Flyingtulip In-Depth Research Report 2026

### DEX / Lending / DeFi
- Uniswap Ecosystem Panoramic Research: AMM to Multi-Chain Infrastructure
- Jupiter Protocol: The Execution Layer Paradox
- Aave Deep Research: Leading Position in the Lending Market
- Maple Finance & SYRUP Token: Investment-Grade Research Report
- Morpho Deep Research: Modular Lending Protocol Analysis
- Superform Omnichain Yield Infrastructure: Deep Research
- Symphony AI-Native DeFi Execution Layer: Investment Analysis
- Sky Token Impact: Emission Cut and Buyback Program Analysis
- Cascade Neo: Cross-Asset Margin Unification Architecture
- TradeGenius: Privacy-First On-Chain Trading OS — YZi Labs Backed
- MetaDAO: Futarchy Mechanism and Market-Driven Governance Analysis
- RaveDAO: Digital-Physical Bridge for Electronic Music Culture & NFTs
- Definitive (EDGE): Non-Custodial Multi-Chain TWAP Trading Terminal

### L1 / L2 / Infrastructure
- Bitcoin: Global Neutral Monetary Network and Digital Reserve Asset Analysis

### ZK / FHE / Privacy
- Aztec Network: Privacy-First zk-Rollup Architecture

- Zama: FHE Stack and the Next Phase of Blockchain Privacy

### Prediction Markets

- The Rise of Prediction Markets: PMF Analysis
- Opinion Labs: Investment Thesis for OPINION Macro Exchange
- Rain Protocol Deep Dive: AMM Prediction Market Infrastructure
- Kairos Deep Dive: Prediction Market Execution Infrastructure
- Predict.fun Deep Research: On-Chain Prediction Markets
- Probable: The Orderbook Oracle On-Chain Prediction Market

### Stablecoin / PayFi
- Circle Global Stablecoin, Cross-Chain Settlement Research
- Global Stablecoin Sector: Investment-Grade Research Report
- RedotPay: Institutional-Grade PayFi Infrastructure Analysis
- USDG (Global Dollar): Institutional-Grade Analysis

- KGST Stablecoin: Investment-Grade Research Report
- Tria Chain-Abstraction Neobank & BestPath AVS Analysis

### AI / DePIN
- Gensyn: Investment-Grade Research Report
- Sentient Protocol: Investment-Grade Analysis & Strategic Assessment

### RWA

- Mobile-First Web3 dApp Era Report (2025)

MIT
